response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship credentials, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ programming; his C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project. His large-scale infrastructure experience (low-latency, millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) is genuine but was in DLang and spanned under 2 years, short of the 5-year requirement. His recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, and he has no formal technical-leadership role leading cloud or platform teams, making him a poor fit for a C++-centric cloud compute platform tech lead position.
strengths: Real high-scale, low-latency systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second)
Strong engineering-practices background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with open-source project ownership
concerns: Far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience (roughly under 1 year professionally)
Large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems experience is under the 5-year minimum and not cloud-compute focused
No formal technical-leadership role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment
